building_name: Red brick church, near Oxford St
facade_colour: dark red brick, aged and weathered

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark red brick, aged and weathered
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Side/rear elevation visible. Brick shows significant weathering and cracking. Vertical crack visible running down the facade. Some repointing evident. White steeple/spire visible above roofline.
photo_observations.window_details: White-framed multi-pane windows with stone or concrete sills and lintels. Windows appear to be replacements in original openings.
photo_observations.door_details: Side entrance visible on this elevation
photo_observations.decorative_details: White steeple/spire rising from roof. Small white plaque or cornerstone on wall. 'NO PARKING' sign on wall.
photo_observations.storefront_description: No storefront; this is a small church building with a side entrance rather than a commercial frontage.
photo_observations.signage_observed: 'NO PARKING' sign. Small white plaque on wall (text not legible).
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement windows. Some brick repair/repointing. Paved parking area at side.
photo_observations.notes: Side/rear elevation of a small red brick church. Only this oblique view available - front facade not captured. White steeple visible. Vertical crack in brickwork suggests some structural movement.

facade_description: Red brick church, near Oxford St presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a gable with white steeple/spire, consistent with its house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using casement, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
